68	        residual = self.bn(residual)

70

72	class TCNStack(nn.Module):
73	    """
74	    Stack of TCN blocks with exponentially increasing dilation.
75	    """
76	
77	    def __init__(self, channels, num_blocks, kernel_size=3, base_dilation=1):
78	        super().__init__()
79	        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList()
80	        for i in range(num_blocks):
81	            dilation = base_dilation * (2 ** i)
82	            self.blocks.append(TCNBlock(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation))
83

85	        skip_sum = 0
86	        for block in self.blocks:
87	            x, skip = block(x)

89	        return x, skip_sum
90

92	class CausalTCNEncoder(nn.Module):
93	    """
94	    Causal TCN encoder that processes mel-spectrogram frames sequentially.
95	
96	    Architecture: input projection → 2 TCN stacks → cumulative mean of
97	    skip connections → fixed-size embedding.
98	
99	    Supports both batch mode (full sequence) and streaming mode (frame-by-frame).
100	    """
101	
102	    def __init__(self, n_mels=128, embedding_dim=128, channels=128,
103	                 num_blocks=4, num_stacks=2, kernel_size=3):
104	        super().__init__()
105	        self.n_mels = n_mels
106	        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
107	        self.channels = channels
108	        self.num_blocks = num_blocks
109	        self.num_stacks = num_stacks
110	
111	        # Input projection: mel bins → channels
112	        self.input_proj = nn.Conv1d(n_mels, channels, 1)
113	
114	        # TCN stacks
115	        self.stacks = nn.ModuleList()
116	        for s in range(num_stacks):
117	            self.stacks.append(TCNStack(channels, num_blocks, kernel_size))
118	
119	        # Output projection
120	        self.output_proj = nn.Linear(channels, embedding_dim)
121	
122	    @property
123	    def receptive_field_frames(self):
124	        """Number of input frames needed for full receptive field."""
125	        # Each block has kernel_size=3, dilation doubles
126	        # Receptive field per block = (kernel_size - 1) * dilation
127	        # Total = sum over all blocks and stacks
128	        rf = 1
129	        for _ in range(self.num_stacks):
130	            for i in range(self.num_blocks):
131	                rf += (3 - 1) * (2 ** i)
132	        return rf
133	
134	    def forward(self, mel_frames):
135	        """
136	        Batch mode: process a sequence of mel frames.
137	
138	        Args:
139	            mel_frames: (Batch, n_mels, T) — sequence of mel-spectrogram frames
140	
141	        Returns:
142	            embedding: (Batch, embedding_dim) — aggregated embedding
143	            skip_sum: (Batch, channels, T) — per-frame skip connections
144	        """
145	        x = self.input_proj(mel_frames)
146	
147	        skip_total = 0
148	        for stack in self.stacks:
149	            x, skip = stack(x)
150	            skip_total = skip_total + skip
151	
152	        # Cumulative mean of skip connections over time
153	        # This gives a stable embedding that improves as more context arrives
154	        cumsum = skip_total.cumsum(dim=-1)
155	        counts = torch.arange(1, skip_total.shape[-1] + 1, device=skip_total.device).float()
156	        cummean = cumsum / counts.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)
157	
158	        # Use the last frame's cumulative mean as the embedding
159	        last_frame = cummean[:, :, -1]  # (Batch, channels)
160	        embedding = self.output_proj(last_frame)  # (Batch, embedding_dim)
161	
162	        return embedding, skip_total
163	
164	
165	class StreamingTCNModel(nn.Module):
166	    """
167	    Complete streaming model for blind EQ parameter estimation.
168	
169	    Combines a causal TCN encoder with multi-type EQ parameter head and
170	    differentiable biquad cascade. Supports both batch training and
171	    streaming inference modes.
172	    """
173	
174	    def __init__(self, n_mels=128, embedding_dim=128, num_bands=5,
175	                 channels=128, num_blocks=4, num_stacks=2,
176	                 sample_rate=44100, n_fft=2048):
177	        super().__init__()
178	        self.n_mels = n_mels
179	        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
180	        self.num_bands = num_bands
181	        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
182	        self.n_fft = n_fft
183	
184	        # Encoder
185	        self.encoder = CausalTCNEncoder(
186	            n_mels=n_mels,
187	            embedding_dim=embedding_dim,
188	            channels=channels,
189	            num_blocks=num_blocks,
190	            num_stacks=num_stacks,
191	        )
192	
193	        # Parameter head
194	        self.param_head = MultiTypeEQParameterHead(embedding_dim, num_bands)
195	
196	        # DSP layer
197	        self.dsp_cascade = DifferentiableBiquadCascade(num_bands, sample_rate)
198	
199	        # Streaming state
200	        self._streaming_buffer = None
201	        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
202	        self._frame_count = 0
203	
204	    @property
205	    def receptive_field_frames(self):
206	        return self.encoder.receptive_field_frames
207	
208	    def reset_state(self):
209	        """Reset streaming state for a new inference session."""
210	        self._streaming_buffer = None
211	        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
212	        self._frame_count = 0
213	
214	    def forward(self, mel_frames):
215	        """
216	        Batch-mode forward pass for training.
217	
218	        Args:
219	            mel_frames: (Batch, n_mels, T) — full mel-spectrogram sequence
220	
221	        Returns:
222	            dict with:
223	                params: (gain_db, freq, q) — each (Batch, num_bands)
224	                type_logits: (Batch, num_bands, 5)
225	                type_probs: (Batch, num_bands, 5)
226	                filter_type: (Batch, num_bands)
227	                H_mag: (Batch, n_fft//2+1) — predicted frequency response
228	                embedding: (Batch, embedding_dim)
229	        """
230	        # 1. Encode
231	        embedding, skip_sum = self.encoder(mel_frames)
232	
233	        # 2. Predict parameters
234	        gain_db, freq, q, type_logits, type_probs, filter_type = self.param_head(
235	            embedding, hard_types=not self.training
236	        )
237	
238	        # 3. Compute frequency response
239	        if self.training:
240	            H_mag = self.dsp_cascade.forward_soft(gain_db, freq, q, type_probs, self.n_fft)
241	        else:
242	            H_mag = self.dsp_cascade(gain_db, freq, q, self.n_fft, filter_type)
243	
244	        # Clamp to prevent NaN in loss
245	        H_mag = H_mag.clamp(min=1e-4)
246	
247	        return {
248	            "params": (gain_db, freq, q),
249	            "type_logits": type_logits,
250	            "type_probs": type_probs,
251	            "filter_type": filter_type,
252	            "H_mag": H_mag,
253	            "embedding": embedding,
254	        }
255	
256	    def init_streaming(self, batch_size=1):
257	        """Initialize streaming state."""
258	        rf = self.receptive_field_frames
259	        self._streaming_buffer = torch.zeros(batch_size, self.n_mels, rf)
260	        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
261	        self._frame_count = 0
262	
263	    def process_frame(self, mel_frame):
264	        """
265	        Streaming inference: process a single mel frame.
266	
267	        Args:
268	            mel_frame: (Batch, n_mels) — single mel-spectrogram frame
269	
270	        Returns:
271	            Same dict as forward(), with parameters estimated from
272	            cumulative context up to this frame.
273	        """
274	        if self._streaming_buffer is None:
275	            self.init_streaming(batch_size=mel_frame.shape[0])
276	
277	        # Shift buffer left and insert new frame
278	        self._streaming_buffer = torch.cat([
279	            self._streaming_buffer[:, :, 1:],
280	            mel_frame.unsqueeze(-1)
281	        ], dim=-1)
282	
283	        self._frame_count += 1
284	
285	        # Run encoder on buffer
286	        x = self.encoder.input_proj(self._streaming_buffer)
287	
288	        skip_total = 0
289	        for stack in self.encoder.stacks:
290	            x, skip = stack(x)
291	            skip_total = skip_total + skip
292	
293	        # Cumulative skip sum (streaming version)
294	        if self._cumulative_skip_sum is None:
295	            self._cumulative_skip_sum = skip_total
296	        else:
297	            self._cumulative_skip_sum = self._cumulative_skip_sum + skip_total
298	
299	        # Use running mean
300	        cummean = self._cumulative_skip_sum / self._frame_count
301	        last_frame = cummean[:, :, -1]
302	        embedding = self.encoder.output_proj(last_frame)
303	
304	        # Predict parameters
305	        gain_db, freq, q, type_logits, type_probs, filter_type = self.param_head(
306	            embedding, hard_types=True
307	        )
308	
309	        H_mag = self.dsp_cascade(gain_db, freq, q, self.n_fft, filter_type)
310	
311	        return {
312	            "params": (gain_db, freq, q),
313	            "type_logits": type_logits,
314	            "type_probs": type_probs,
315	            "filter_type": filter_type,
316	            "H_mag": H_mag,
317	            "embedding": embedding,
318	        }
319

"""
Streaming-compatible TCN encoder for blind EQ parameter estimation.

Uses causal 1D convolutions with dilated temporal convolutional network (TCN)
architecture for real-time audio processing. Based on WaveNet-style gated
activations with residual and skip connections.
"""
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from differentiable_eq import DifferentiableBiquadCascade, MultiTypeEQParameterHead


class CausalConv1d(nn.Module):
    """Causal 1D convolution with left-only padding (no look-ahead)."""

    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, dilation=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.padding = (kernel_size - 1) * dilation
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size,
            dilation=dilation, padding=0  # We handle padding manually
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        # Left-only padding for causality
        x = F.pad(x, (self.padding, 0))
        return self.conv(x)


class GatedResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    """
    Gated activation block with residual and skip connections.
    Uses tanh * sigmoid gating (WaveNet-style).
    """

    def __init__(self, channels, kernel_size=3, dilation=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.filter_conv = CausalConv1d(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation)
        self.gate_conv = CausalConv1d(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation)

    def forward(self, x):
        z_filter = torch.tanh(self.filter_conv(x))
        z_gate = torch.sigmoid(self.gate_conv(x))
        return z_filter * z_gate


class TCNBlock(nn.Module):
    """
    Single TCN block: gated residual + 1x1 conv for skip/residual.
    """

    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size=3, dilation=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.gated_block = GatedResidualBlock(in_channels, kernel_size, dilation)

        # 1x1 convolutions for residual and skip connections
        self.residual_conv = nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, 1) \
            if in_channels != out_channels else nn.Identity()
        self.skip_conv = nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, 1)

        self.bn = nn.BatchNorm1d(out_channels)

    def forward(self, x):
        gated = self.gated_block(x)
        skip = self.skip_conv(gated)
        residual = self.residual_conv(x) + gated
        residual = self.bn(residual)
        return residual, skip


class TCNStack(nn.Module):
    """
    Stack of TCN blocks with exponentially increasing dilation.
    """

    def __init__(self, channels, num_blocks, kernel_size=3, base_dilation=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList()
        for i in range(num_blocks):
            dilation = base_dilation * (2 ** i)
            self.blocks.append(TCNBlock(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation))

    def forward(self, x):
        skip_sum = 0
        for block in self.blocks:
            x, skip = block(x)
            skip_sum = skip_sum + skip
        return x, skip_sum


class CausalTCNEncoder(nn.Module):
    """
    Causal TCN encoder that processes mel-spectrogram frames sequentially.

    Architecture: input projection → 2 TCN stacks → cumulative mean of
    skip connections → fixed-size embedding.

    Supports both batch mode (full sequence) and streaming mode (frame-by-frame).
    """

    def __init__(self, n_mels=128, embedding_dim=128, channels=128,
                 num_blocks=4, num_stacks=2, kernel_size=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_mels = n_mels
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
        self.channels = channels
        self.num_blocks = num_blocks
        self.num_stacks = num_stacks

        # Input projection: mel bins → channels
        self.input_proj = nn.Conv1d(n_mels, channels, 1)

        # TCN stacks
        self.stacks = nn.ModuleList()
        for s in range(num_stacks):
            self.stacks.append(TCNStack(channels, num_blocks, kernel_size))

        # Output projection
        self.output_proj = nn.Linear(channels, embedding_dim)

    @property
    def receptive_field_frames(self):
        """Number of input frames needed for full receptive field."""
        # Each block has kernel_size=3, dilation doubles
        # Receptive field per block = (kernel_size - 1) * dilation
        # Total = sum over all blocks and stacks
        rf = 1
        for _ in range(self.num_stacks):
            for i in range(self.num_blocks):
                rf += (3 - 1) * (2 ** i)
        return rf

    def forward(self, mel_frames):
        """
        Batch mode: process a sequence of mel frames.

        Args:
            mel_frames: (Batch, n_mels, T) — sequence of mel-spectrogram frames

        Returns:
            embedding: (Batch, embedding_dim) — aggregated embedding
            skip_sum: (Batch, channels, T) — per-frame skip connections
        """
        x = self.input_proj(mel_frames)

        skip_total = 0
        for stack in self.stacks:
            x, skip = stack(x)
            skip_total = skip_total + skip

        # Cumulative mean of skip connections over time
        # This gives a stable embedding that improves as more context arrives
        cumsum = skip_total.cumsum(dim=-1)
        counts = torch.arange(1, skip_total.shape[-1] + 1, device=skip_total.device).float()
        cummean = cumsum / counts.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)

        # Use the last frame's cumulative mean as the embedding
        last_frame = cummean[:, :, -1]  # (Batch, channels)
        embedding = self.output_proj(last_frame)  # (Batch, embedding_dim)

        return embedding, skip_total


class StreamingTCNModel(nn.Module):
    """
    Complete streaming model for blind EQ parameter estimation.

    Combines a causal TCN encoder with multi-type EQ parameter head and
    differentiable biquad cascade. Supports both batch training and
    streaming inference modes.
    """

    def __init__(self, n_mels=128, embedding_dim=128, num_bands=5,
                 channels=128, num_blocks=4, num_stacks=2,
                 sample_rate=44100, n_fft=2048):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_mels = n_mels
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
        self.num_bands = num_bands
        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
        self.n_fft = n_fft

        # Encoder
        self.encoder = CausalTCNEncoder(
            n_mels=n_mels,
            embedding_dim=embedding_dim,
            channels=channels,
            num_blocks=num_blocks,
            num_stacks=num_stacks,
        )

        # Parameter head
        self.param_head = MultiTypeEQParameterHead(embedding_dim, num_bands)

        # DSP layer
        self.dsp_cascade = DifferentiableBiquadCascade(num_bands, sample_rate)

        # Streaming state
        self._streaming_buffer = None
        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
        self._frame_count = 0

    @property
    def receptive_field_frames(self):
        return self.encoder.receptive_field_frames

    def reset_state(self):
        """Reset streaming state for a new inference session."""
        self._streaming_buffer = None
        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
        self._frame_count = 0

    def forward(self, mel_frames):
        """
        Batch-mode forward pass for training.

        Args:
            mel_frames: (Batch, n_mels, T) — full mel-spectrogram sequence

        Returns:
            dict with:
                params: (gain_db, freq, q) — each (Batch, num_bands)
                type_logits: (Batch, num_bands, 5)
                type_probs: (Batch, num_bands, 5)
                filter_type: (Batch, num_bands)
                H_mag: (Batch, n_fft//2+1) — predicted frequency response
                embedding: (Batch, embedding_dim)
        """
        # 1. Encode
        embedding, skip_sum = self.encoder(mel_frames)

        # 2. Predict parameters
        gain_db, freq, q, type_logits, type_probs, filter_type = self.param_head(
            embedding, hard_types=not self.training
        )

        # 3. Compute frequency response
        if self.training:
            H_mag = self.dsp_cascade.forward_soft(gain_db, freq, q, type_probs, self.n_fft)
        else:
            H_mag = self.dsp_cascade(gain_db, freq, q, self.n_fft, filter_type)

        # Clamp to prevent NaN in loss
        H_mag = H_mag.clamp(min=1e-4)

        return {
            "params": (gain_db, freq, q),
            "type_logits": type_logits,
            "type_probs": type_probs,
            "filter_type": filter_type,
            "H_mag": H_mag,
            "embedding": embedding,
        }

    def init_streaming(self, batch_size=1):
        """Initialize streaming state."""
        rf = self.receptive_field_frames
        self._streaming_buffer = torch.zeros(batch_size, self.n_mels, rf)
        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
        self._frame_count = 0

    def process_frame(self, mel_frame):
        """
        Streaming inference: process a single mel frame.

        Args:
            mel_frame: (Batch, n_mels) — single mel-spectrogram frame

        Returns:
            Same dict as forward(), with parameters estimated from
            cumulative context up to this frame.
        """
        if self._streaming_buffer is None:
            self.init_streaming(batch_size=mel_frame.shape[0])

        # Shift buffer left and insert new frame
        self._streaming_buffer = torch.cat([
            self._streaming_buffer[:, :, 1:],
            mel_frame.unsqueeze(-1)
        ], dim=-1)

        self._frame_count += 1

        # Run encoder on buffer
        x = self.encoder.input_proj(self._streaming_buffer)

        skip_total = 0
        for stack in self.encoder.stacks:
            x, skip = stack(x)
            skip_total = skip_total + skip

        # Cumulative skip sum (streaming version)
        if self._cumulative_skip_sum is None:
            self._cumulative_skip_sum = skip_total
        else:
            self._cumulative_skip_sum = self._cumulative_skip_sum + skip_total

        # Use running mean
        cummean = self._cumulative_skip_sum / self._frame_count
        last_frame = cummean[:, :, -1]
        embedding = self.encoder.output_proj(last_frame)

        # Predict parameters
        gain_db, freq, q, type_logits, type_probs, filter_type = self.param_head(
            embedding, hard_types=True
        )

        H_mag = self.dsp_cascade(gain_db, freq, q, self.n_fft, filter_type)

        return {
            "params": (gain_db, freq, q),
            "type_logits": type_logits,
            "type_probs": type_probs,
            "filter_type": filter_type,
            "H_mag": H_mag,
            "embedding": embedding,
        }

1	"""
2	Multi-type EQ estimation loss functions.
3	
4	Key components:
5	- Hungarian matching for permutation-invariant band assignment (DETR-style)
6	- Combined regression + classification loss
7	- Spectral consistency loss for blind estimation
8	- Auxiliary losses (band activity, frequency spread)
9	"""
10	import torch
11	import torch.nn as nn
12	import torch.nn.functional as F
13	from scipy.optimize import linear_sum_assignment
14	import numpy as np
15	
16	from loss import MultiResolutionSTFTLoss
17	
18	
19	class HungarianBandMatcher:
20	    """
21	    Solves the band permutation problem using Hungarian (bipartite) matching.
22	
23	    When predicting N EQ bands, the model's band ordering is arbitrary.
24	    This finds the optimal assignment between predicted and ground-truth bands
25	    to minimize parameter distance, following Carion et al. (DETR, 2020).
26	    """
27	
28	    def __init__(self, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5, lambda_type_match=2.0):
29	        self.lambda_freq = lambda_freq
30	        self.lambda_q = lambda_q
31	        self.lambda_type_match = lambda_type_match
32	
33	    def compute_cost_matrix(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
34	                            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
35	                            pred_type_logits=None, target_filter_type=None):
36	        """
37	        Compute pairwise cost matrix between predicted and target bands.
38	
39	        All param inputs: (Batch, Num_Bands)
40	        pred_type_logits: optional (B, N, C) for soft type matching
41	        target_filter_type: optional (B, N) for type mismatch penalty
42	        Returns: cost matrix (Batch, Num_Pred, Num_Target)
43	        """
44	        B, N = pred_gain.shape
45	
46	        # Expand for pairwise computation: (B, N, 1) vs (B, 1, N)
47	        pg = pred_gain.unsqueeze(-1)      # (B, N, 1)
48	        tg = target_gain.unsqueeze(-2)    # (B, 1, N)
49	        pf = pred_freq.unsqueeze(-1)
50	        tf = target_freq.unsqueeze(-2)
51	        pq = pred_q.unsqueeze(-1)
52	        tq = target_q.unsqueeze(-2)
53	
54	        # Gain cost: L1 in dB
55	        cost_gain = (pg - tg).abs()
56	
57	        # Frequency cost: L1 in log-space (octaves)
58	        cost_freq = self.lambda_freq * (torch.log(pf.clamp(min=1e-4)) - torch.log(tf.clamp(min=1e-4))).abs()
59	
60	        # Q cost: L1 in log-space (decades)
61	        cost_q = self.lambda_q * (torch.log(pq.clamp(min=1e-4)) - torch.log(tq.clamp(min=1e-4))).abs()
62	
63	        cost = cost_gain + cost_freq + cost_q  # (B, N, N)
64	
65	        # Type mismatch penalty: prefer matching bands of the same filter type
66	        if pred_type_logits is not None and target_filter_type is not None:
67	            # Use predicted type probabilities for soft matching
68	            pred_type_probs = torch.softmax(pred_type_logits, dim=-1)  # (B, N, C)
69	            # For each target type, cost = 1 - predicted probability of that type
70	            pt = pred_type_probs.unsqueeze(-2)  # (B, N, 1, C)
71	            tt_idx = target_filter_type.unsqueeze(-1)  # (B, 1, N)
72	            # Gather predicted prob for the target type
73	            pt_expanded = pt.expand(B, N, N, pt.shape[-1])  # (B, N, N, C)
74	            tt_expanded = tt_idx.expand(B, N, N)  # (B, N, N)
75	            pred_prob_for_target = pt_expanded.gather(-1, tt_expanded.unsqueeze(-1)).squeeze(-1)  # (B, N, N)
76	            cost_type = self.lambda_type_match * (1.0 - pred_prob_for_target)
77	            cost = cost + cost_type
78	
79	        return cost
80	
81	    def match(self, cost_matrix):
82	        """
83	        Solve the assignment problem for each batch element.
84	
85	        Args:
86	            cost_matrix: (Batch, N_pred, N_target)
87	
88	        Returns:
89	            List of (row_indices, col_indices) tuples per batch element
90	        """
91	        B = cost_matrix.shape[0]
92	        assignments = []
93	        for b in range(B):
94	            cost_np = cost_matrix[b].detach().cpu().numpy()
95	            # Replace NaN/Inf with large finite value so Hungarian matching works
96	            cost_np = np.nan_to_num(cost_np, nan=1e6, posinf=1e6, neginf=-1e6)
97	            row_ind, col_ind = linear_sum_assignment(cost_np)
98	            assignments.append((row_ind, col_ind))
99	        return assignments
100	
101	    def __call__(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
102	                 target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
103	                 pred_type_logits=None, target_filter_type=None):
104	        """
105	        Find optimal matching and reorder targets.
106	
107	        Returns:
108	            target_gain_matched, target_freq_matched, target_q_matched
109	            — ground truth reordered to match predicted band ordering
110	        """
111	        cost = self.compute_cost_matrix(
112	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
113	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
114	            pred_type_logits, target_filter_type,
115	        )
116	        assignments = self.match(cost)
117	
118	        B, N = pred_gain.shape
119	        device = pred_gain.device
120	
121	        matched_gain = torch.zeros_like(target_gain)
122	        matched_freq = torch.zeros_like(target_freq)
123	        matched_q = torch.zeros_like(target_q)
124	        matched_type = torch.zeros_like(target_filter_type) if target_filter_type is not None else None
125	
126	        for b in range(B):
127	            row_ind, col_ind = assignments[b]
128	            perm = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.long, device=device)
129	            for r, c in zip(row_ind, col_ind):
130	                perm[r] = c
131	            matched_gain[b] = target_gain[b, perm]
132	            matched_freq[b] = target_freq[b, perm]
133	            matched_q[b] = target_q[b, perm]
134	            if matched_type is not None:
135	                matched_type[b] = target_filter_type[b, perm]
136	
137	        if matched_type is not None:
138	            return matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q, matched_type
139	        return matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q
140	
141	
142	class PermutationInvariantParamLoss(nn.Module):
143	    """
144	    Parameter regression loss with Hungarian matching for band ordering.
145	    Uses Huber loss for robustness to outliers.
146	    """
147	
148	    def __init__(self, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5):
149	        super().__init__()
150	        self.matcher = HungarianBandMatcher(lambda_freq, lambda_q)
151	        self.huber = nn.HuberLoss(delta=1.0)
152	
153	    def forward(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
154	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
155	                pred_type_logits=None, target_filter_type=None):
156	        """
157	        All inputs: (Batch, Num_Bands)
158	        Returns: scalar loss, matched_type (or None)
159	        """
160	        match_result = self.matcher(
161	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
162	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
163	            pred_type_logits, target_filter_type,
164	        )
165	        if len(match_result) == 4:
166	            matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q, matched_type = match_result
167	        else:
168	            matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q = match_result
169	            matched_type = None
170	
171	        loss_gain = self.huber(pred_gain, matched_gain)
172	        loss_freq = self.huber(
173	            torch.log(pred_freq + 1e-8),
174	            torch.log(matched_freq + 1e-8)
175	        )
176	        loss_q = self.huber(
177	            torch.log(pred_q + 1e-8),
178	            torch.log(matched_q + 1e-8)
179	        )
180	
181	        return loss_gain + loss_freq + loss_q, matched_type
182	
183	
184	class MultiTypeEQLoss(nn.Module):
185	    """
186	    Combined loss for multi-type blind EQ estimation.
187	
188	    Components:
189	    - L_param: Permutation-invariant parameter regression (Huber)
190	    - L_type: Filter type classification (cross-entropy)
191	    - L_spectral: MR-STFT spectral consistency
192	    - L_hmag: Frequency response magnitude L1
193	    - L_activity: Band activity regularization
194	    - L_spread: Frequency spread regularization
195	    """
196	
197	    def __init__(self, n_fft=2048, sample_rate=44100,
198	                 lambda_param=1.0, lambda_type=2.0,
199	                 lambda_spectral=1.0, lambda_hmag=0.3,
200	                 lambda_activity=0.1, lambda_spread=0.05):
201	        super().__init__()
202	        self.lambda_param = lambda_param
203	        self.lambda_type = lambda_type
204	        self.lambda_spectral = lambda_spectral
205	        self.lambda_hmag = lambda_hmag
206	        self.lambda_activity = lambda_activity
207	        self.lambda_spread = lambda_spread
208	
209	        self.param_loss = PermutationInvariantParamLoss()
210	        # Class weights: upweight HP/LP which are harder to distinguish from peaking
211	        # Registered as buffer so it moves with .to(device)
212	        self.register_buffer(
213	            "type_class_weights",
214	            torch.tensor([1.0, 1.5, 1.5, 2.0, 2.0])
215	        )
216	        self.mr_stft = MultiResolutionSTFTLoss()
217	        self.huber = nn.HuberLoss()
218	
219	    def forward(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
220	                pred_type_logits, pred_H_mag,
221	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
222	                target_filter_type, target_H_mag,
223	                pred_audio=None, target_audio=None,
224	                active_band_mask=None):
225	        """
226	        Compute total loss.
227	
228	        Args:
229	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q: (B, N) predicted parameters
230	            pred_type_logits: (B, N, 5) predicted type logits
231	            pred_H_mag: (B, F) predicted frequency response
232	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q: (B, N) ground truth params
233	            target_filter_type: (B, N) ground truth type indices
234	            target_H_mag: (B, F) ground truth frequency response
235	            pred_audio: (B, T) optional reconstructed audio
236	            target_audio: (B, T) optional target audio
237	            active_band_mask: (B, N) optional mask for active bands
238	
239	        Returns:
240	            total_loss: scalar
241	            components: dict of individual loss values
242	        """
243	        components = {}
244	
245	        # 1. Parameter regression (permutation-invariant, type-aware matching)
246	        loss_param, matched_type = self.param_loss(
247	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
248	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
249	            pred_type_logits, target_filter_type,
250	        )
251	        components["param_loss"] = loss_param
252	
253	        # 2. Filter type classification (use matched targets for cleaner gradients)
254	        B, N, C = pred_type_logits.shape
255	        type_target = matched_type if matched_type is not None else target_filter_type
256	        loss_type = F.cross_entropy(
257	            pred_type_logits.reshape(B * N, C),
258	            type_target.reshape(B * N),
259	            weight=self.type_class_weights,
260	        )
261	        components["type_loss"] = loss_type
262	
263	        # 3. Frequency response magnitude L1
264	        loss_hmag = F.l1_loss(
265	            torch.log(pred_H_mag.clamp(min=1e-4)),
266	            torch.log(target_H_mag.clamp(min=1e-4))
267	        )
268	        components["hmag_loss"] = loss_hmag
269	
270	        # 4. Spectral consistency (if audio provided)
271	        if pred_audio is not None and target_audio is not None:
272	            sc_loss, log_loss = self.mr_stft(pred_audio, target_audio)
273	            loss_spectral = sc_loss + log_loss
274	            components["spectral_loss"] = loss_spectral
275	        else:
276	            loss_spectral = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
277	            components["spectral_loss"] = loss_spectral
278	
279	        # 5. Band activity regularization
280	        if active_band_mask is not None:
281	            inactive_mask = ~active_band_mask
282	            loss_activity = (pred_gain * inactive_mask.float()).abs().mean()
283	        else:
284	            loss_activity = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
285	        components["activity_loss"] = loss_activity
286	
287	        # 6. Frequency spread regularization
288	        if pred_freq.shape[1] > 1:
289	            log_freq = torch.log(pred_freq + 1e-8)
290	            # Pairwise log-frequency differences
291	            diff = log_freq.unsqueeze(-1) - log_freq.unsqueeze(-2)  # (B, N, N)
292	            spread = diff.abs().mean()
293	            loss_spread = -spread  # Maximize spread
294	        else:
295	            loss_spread = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
296	        components["spread_loss"] = loss_spread
297	
298	        # Total
299	        total_loss = (
300	            self.lambda_param * loss_param
301	            + self.lambda_type * loss_type
302	            + self.lambda_spectral * loss_spectral
303	            + self.lambda_hmag * loss_hmag
304	            + self.lambda_activity * loss_activity
305	            + self.lambda_spread * loss_spread
306	        )
307	
308	        return total_loss, components
309

"""
Multi-type EQ estimation loss functions.

Key components:
- Hungarian matching for permutation-invariant band assignment (DETR-style)
- Combined regression + classification loss
- Spectral consistency loss for blind estimation
- Auxiliary losses (band activity, frequency spread)
"""
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from scipy.optimize import linear_sum_assignment
import numpy as np

from loss import MultiResolutionSTFTLoss


class HungarianBandMatcher:
    """
    Solves the band permutation problem using Hungarian (bipartite) matching.

    When predicting N EQ bands, the model's band ordering is arbitrary.
    This finds the optimal assignment between predicted and ground-truth bands
    to minimize parameter distance, following Carion et al. (DETR, 2020).
    """

    def __init__(self, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5, lambda_type_match=2.0):
        self.lambda_freq = lambda_freq
        self.lambda_q = lambda_q
        self.lambda_type_match = lambda_type_match

    def compute_cost_matrix(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
                            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
                            pred_type_logits=None, target_filter_type=None):
        """
        Compute pairwise cost matrix between predicted and target bands.

        All param inputs: (Batch, Num_Bands)
        pred_type_logits: optional (B, N, C) for soft type matching
        target_filter_type: optional (B, N) for type mismatch penalty
        Returns: cost matrix (Batch, Num_Pred, Num_Target)
        """
        B, N = pred_gain.shape

        # Expand for pairwise computation: (B, N, 1) vs (B, 1, N)
        pg = pred_gain.unsqueeze(-1)      # (B, N, 1)
        tg = target_gain.unsqueeze(-2)    # (B, 1, N)
        pf = pred_freq.unsqueeze(-1)
        tf = target_freq.unsqueeze(-2)
        pq = pred_q.unsqueeze(-1)
        tq = target_q.unsqueeze(-2)

        # Gain cost: L1 in dB
        cost_gain = (pg - tg).abs()

        # Frequency cost: L1 in log-space (octaves)
        cost_freq = self.lambda_freq * (torch.log(pf.clamp(min=1e-4)) - torch.log(tf.clamp(min=1e-4))).abs()

        # Q cost: L1 in log-space (decades)
        cost_q = self.lambda_q * (torch.log(pq.clamp(min=1e-4)) - torch.log(tq.clamp(min=1e-4))).abs()

        cost = cost_gain + cost_freq + cost_q  # (B, N, N)

        # Type mismatch penalty: prefer matching bands of the same filter type
        if pred_type_logits is not None and target_filter_type is not None:
            # Use predicted type probabilities for soft matching
            pred_type_probs = torch.softmax(pred_type_logits, dim=-1)  # (B, N, C)
            # For each target type, cost = 1 - predicted probability of that type
            pt = pred_type_probs.unsqueeze(-2)  # (B, N, 1, C)
            tt_idx = target_filter_type.unsqueeze(-1)  # (B, 1, N)
            # Gather predicted prob for the target type
            pt_expanded = pt.expand(B, N, N, pt.shape[-1])  # (B, N, N, C)
            tt_expanded = tt_idx.expand(B, N, N)  # (B, N, N)
            pred_prob_for_target = pt_expanded.gather(-1, tt_expanded.unsqueeze(-1)).squeeze(-1)  # (B, N, N)
            cost_type = self.lambda_type_match * (1.0 - pred_prob_for_target)
            cost = cost + cost_type

        return cost

    def match(self, cost_matrix):
        """
        Solve the assignment problem for each batch element.

        Args:
            cost_matrix: (Batch, N_pred, N_target)

        Returns:
            List of (row_indices, col_indices) tuples per batch element
        """
        B = cost_matrix.shape[0]
        assignments = []
        for b in range(B):
            cost_np = cost_matrix[b].detach().cpu().numpy()
            # Replace NaN/Inf with large finite value so Hungarian matching works
            cost_np = np.nan_to_num(cost_np, nan=1e6, posinf=1e6, neginf=-1e6)
            row_ind, col_ind = linear_sum_assignment(cost_np)
            assignments.append((row_ind, col_ind))
        return assignments

    def __call__(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
                 target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
                 pred_type_logits=None, target_filter_type=None):
        """
        Find optimal matching and reorder targets.

        Returns:
            target_gain_matched, target_freq_matched, target_q_matched
            — ground truth reordered to match predicted band ordering
        """
        cost = self.compute_cost_matrix(
            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
            pred_type_logits, target_filter_type,
        )
        assignments = self.match(cost)

        B, N = pred_gain.shape
        device = pred_gain.device

        matched_gain = torch.zeros_like(target_gain)
        matched_freq = torch.zeros_like(target_freq)
        matched_q = torch.zeros_like(target_q)
        matched_type = torch.zeros_like(target_filter_type) if target_filter_type is not None else None

        for b in range(B):
            row_ind, col_ind = assignments[b]
            perm = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.long, device=device)
            for r, c in zip(row_ind, col_ind):
                perm[r] = c
            matched_gain[b] = target_gain[b, perm]
            matched_freq[b] = target_freq[b, perm]
            matched_q[b] = target_q[b, perm]
            if matched_type is not None:
                matched_type[b] = target_filter_type[b, perm]

        if matched_type is not None:
            return matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q, matched_type
        return matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q


class PermutationInvariantParamLoss(nn.Module):
    """
    Parameter regression loss with Hungarian matching for band ordering.
    Uses Huber loss for robustness to outliers.
    """

    def __init__(self, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5):
        super().__init__()
        self.matcher = HungarianBandMatcher(lambda_freq, lambda_q)
        self.huber = nn.HuberLoss(delta=1.0)

    def forward(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
                pred_type_logits=None, target_filter_type=None):
        """
        All inputs: (Batch, Num_Bands)
        Returns: scalar loss, matched_type (or None)
        """
        match_result = self.matcher(
            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
            pred_type_logits, target_filter_type,
        )
        if len(match_result) == 4:
            matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q, matched_type = match_result
        else:
            matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q = match_result
            matched_type = None

        loss_gain = self.huber(pred_gain, matched_gain)
        loss_freq = self.huber(
            torch.log(pred_freq + 1e-8),
            torch.log(matched_freq + 1e-8)
        )
        loss_q = self.huber(
            torch.log(pred_q + 1e-8),
            torch.log(matched_q + 1e-8)
        )

        return loss_gain + loss_freq + loss_q, matched_type


class MultiTypeEQLoss(nn.Module):
    """
    Combined loss for multi-type blind EQ estimation.

    Components:
    - L_param: Permutation-invariant parameter regression (Huber)
    - L_type: Filter type classification (cross-entropy)
    - L_spectral: MR-STFT spectral consistency
    - L_hmag: Frequency response magnitude L1
    - L_activity: Band activity regularization
    - L_spread: Frequency spread regularization
    """

    def __init__(self, n_fft=2048, sample_rate=44100,
                 lambda_param=1.0, lambda_type=2.0,
                 lambda_spectral=1.0, lambda_hmag=0.3,
                 lambda_activity=0.1, lambda_spread=0.05):
        super().__init__()
        self.lambda_param = lambda_param
        self.lambda_type = lambda_type
        self.lambda_spectral = lambda_spectral
        self.lambda_hmag = lambda_hmag
        self.lambda_activity = lambda_activity
        self.lambda_spread = lambda_spread

        self.param_loss = PermutationInvariantParamLoss()
        # Class weights: upweight HP/LP which are harder to distinguish from peaking
        # Registered as buffer so it moves with .to(device)
        self.register_buffer(
            "type_class_weights",
            torch.tensor([1.0, 1.5, 1.5, 2.0, 2.0])
        )
        self.mr_stft = MultiResolutionSTFTLoss()
        self.huber = nn.HuberLoss()

    def forward(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
                pred_type_logits, pred_H_mag,
                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
                target_filter_type, target_H_mag,
                pred_audio=None, target_audio=None,
                active_band_mask=None):
        """
        Compute total loss.

        Args:
            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q: (B, N) predicted parameters
            pred_type_logits: (B, N, 5) predicted type logits
            pred_H_mag: (B, F) predicted frequency response
            target_gain, target_freq, target_q: (B, N) ground truth params
            target_filter_type: (B, N) ground truth type indices
            target_H_mag: (B, F) ground truth frequency response
            pred_audio: (B, T) optional reconstructed audio
            target_audio: (B, T) optional target audio
            active_band_mask: (B, N) optional mask for active bands

        Returns:
            total_loss: scalar
            components: dict of individual loss values
        """
        components = {}

        # 1. Parameter regression (permutation-invariant, type-aware matching)
        loss_param, matched_type = self.param_loss(
            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
            pred_type_logits, target_filter_type,
        )
        components["param_loss"] = loss_param

        # 2. Filter type classification (use matched targets for cleaner gradients)
        B, N, C = pred_type_logits.shape
        type_target = matched_type if matched_type is not None else target_filter_type
        loss_type = F.cross_entropy(
            pred_type_logits.reshape(B * N, C),
            type_target.reshape(B * N),
            weight=self.type_class_weights,
        )
        components["type_loss"] = loss_type

        # 3. Frequency response magnitude L1
        loss_hmag = F.l1_loss(
            torch.log(pred_H_mag.clamp(min=1e-4)),
            torch.log(target_H_mag.clamp(min=1e-4))
        )
        components["hmag_loss"] = loss_hmag

        # 4. Spectral consistency (if audio provided)
        if pred_audio is not None and target_audio is not None:
            sc_loss, log_loss = self.mr_stft(pred_audio, target_audio)
            loss_spectral = sc_loss + log_loss
            components["spectral_loss"] = loss_spectral
        else:
            loss_spectral = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
            components["spectral_loss"] = loss_spectral

        # 5. Band activity regularization
        if active_band_mask is not None:
            inactive_mask = ~active_band_mask
            loss_activity = (pred_gain * inactive_mask.float()).abs().mean()
        else:
            loss_activity = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
        components["activity_loss"] = loss_activity

        # 6. Frequency spread regularization
        if pred_freq.shape[1] > 1:
            log_freq = torch.log(pred_freq + 1e-8)
            # Pairwise log-frequency differences
            diff = log_freq.unsqueeze(-1) - log_freq.unsqueeze(-2)  # (B, N, N)
            spread = diff.abs().mean()
            loss_spread = -spread  # Maximize spread
        else:
            loss_spread = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
        components["spread_loss"] = loss_spread

        # Total
        total_loss = (
            self.lambda_param * loss_param
            + self.lambda_type * loss_type
            + self.lambda_spectral * loss_spectral
            + self.lambda_hmag * loss_hmag
            + self.lambda_activity * loss_activity
            + self.lambda_spread * loss_spread
        )

        return total_loss, components

8	import torch

69

76	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,

78	
79	        # Loss (base weights — curriculum stages can override)
80	        self.base_loss_cfg = loss_cfg
81	        self.criterion = MultiTypeEQLoss(
82	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
83	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
84	            lambda_param=loss_cfg.get("lambda_param", 1.0),
85	            lambda_type=loss_cfg.get("lambda_type", 2.0),
86	            lambda_spectral=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spectral", 1.0),
87	            lambda_hmag=loss_cfg.get("lambda_hmag", 0.3),
88	            lambda_activity=loss_cfg.get("lambda_activity", 0.1),
89	            lambda_spread=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spread", 0.05),
90	        ).to(self.device)
91	
92	        self.optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
93	            self.model.parameters(),
94	            lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
95	            weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
96	        )
97	
98	        self.data_cfg = data_cfg
99	        self.global_step = 0
100	        self.best_val_loss = float("inf")

117	    def _build_stage_dataset(self, stage_cfg):
118	        """Build a dataset for a specific curriculum stage."""
119	        data_cfg = self.data_cfg
120	
121	        # Determine which filter types to include
122	        filter_types = stage_cfg.get("filter_types", data_cfg["filter_types"])
123	        # Build type_weights: uniform over active types, 0 for inactive
124	        active_indices = [FILTER_TYPE_TO_IDX[ft] for ft in filter_types]
125	        n_active = len(active_indices)

129	
130	        # Get param ranges for this stage (or fall back to data config)

133	        # q is a list of [lo, hi]
134	        q_range = param_ranges.get("q", data_cfg["q_bounds"])
135	
136	        # Read duration and size from config instead of hardcoding

138	        size = data_cfg.get("dataset_size", 50000)

145	            size=size,
146	            gain_range=(gain_lo, gain_hi),
147	            freq_range=tuple(data_cfg["freq_bounds"]),
148	            q_range=tuple(q_range),
149	            type_weights=type_weights,
150	            precompute_mels=True,
151	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
152	        )
153	        dataset.precompute()
154	        return dataset
155	
156	    def _setup_stage(self, stage_cfg):
157	        """Prepare dataset and update loss weights for a curriculum stage."""
158	        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
159	        print(f"  Stage: {stage_cfg['name']}")
160	        print(f"{'='*60}")
161	
162	        # CRITICAL FIX: Always rebuild dataset for each curriculum stage
163	        # with stage-specific param ranges. Free old loaders first.
164	        if self.train_loader is not None:
165	            print("  Freeing previous stage data loaders...")
166	            del self.train_loader
167	            del self.val_loader
168	            gc.collect()
169	
170	        dataset = self._build_stage_dataset(stage_cfg)
171	
172	        # Print confirmation of actual param ranges from this stage's dataset
173	        param_ranges = stage_cfg.get("param_ranges", {})
174	        if "gain" in param_ranges:
175	            print(f"  Stage gain range: {param_ranges['gain']}")
176	        if "q" in param_ranges:
177	            print(f"  Stage Q range: {param_ranges['q']}")
178	        print(f"  Dataset size: {len(dataset)} samples")
179	
180	        n_train = int(len(dataset) * self.data_cfg["train_split"])
181	        n_val = int(len(dataset) * self.data_cfg["val_split"])
182	        n_test = len(dataset) - n_train - n_val
183	
184	        # Seeded generator for reproducible splits
185	        gen = torch.Generator().manual_seed(self.cfg.get("seed", 42))
186	        train_set, val_set, test_set = torch.utils.data.random_split(
187	            dataset, [n_train, n_val, n_test], generator=gen
188	        )
189	
190	        # Read num_workers from config instead of hardcoding 0
191	        num_workers = self.data_cfg.get("num_workers", 0)
192	        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
193	
194	        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
195	            train_set,
196	            batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
197	            shuffle=True,
198	            num_workers=num_workers,
199	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
200	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
201	            drop_last=True,
202	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
203	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
204	        )
205	        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
206	            val_set,
207	            batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
208	            shuffle=False,
209	            num_workers=num_workers,
210	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
211	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
212	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
213	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
214	        )
215	
216	        # Update loss weights from stage config
217	        if "lambda_type" in stage_cfg:
218	            self.criterion.lambda_type = stage_cfg["lambda_type"]
219	            print(f"  lambda_type = {stage_cfg['lambda_type']}")
220	
221	        # Update Gumbel temperature if model supports it

223	        if gumbel_temp is not None and hasattr(self.model, 'dsp_cascade'):
224	            if hasattr(self.model.dsp_cascade, 'gumbel_temperature'):
225	                self.model.dsp_cascade.gumbel_temperature.fill_(gumbel_temp)
226	                print(f"  gumbel_temperature = {gumbel_temp}")
227	
228	        # Update learning rate scale for fine-tuning stages
229	        lr_scale = stage_cfg.get("learning_rate_scale", None)
230	        if lr_scale is not None:
231	            for pg in self.optimizer.param_groups:
232	                pg['lr'] = self.cfg["model"]["learning_rate"] * lr_scale
233	            print(f"  lr scaled to {self.optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr']:.2e}")
234	
235	        # Rebuild scheduler for this stage's epoch count
236	        stage_epochs = stage_cfg["epochs"]
237	        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(

239	        )

241	    def _prepare_input(self, batch):
242	        """Get mel-spectrogram from precomputed cache or compute on-the-fly."""
243	        if "wet_mel" in batch:
244	            return batch["wet_mel"].to(self.device)
245	        wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"].to(self.device)
246	        mel_spec = self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio)
247	        return mel_spec.squeeze(1)

249	    def train_one_epoch(self, epoch):
250	        self.model.train()
251	        epoch_loss = 0.0
252	        n_batches = 0
253	
254	        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(self.train_loader):
255	            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
256	            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device)
257	            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device)
258	            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device)
259	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device)
260	
261	            self.optimizer.zero_grad()
262	
263	            # Mixed precision training with AMP
264	            if self.use_amp:
265	                with torch.amp.autocast("cuda", enabled=self.use_amp):
266	                    output = self.model(mel_frames)
267	                    pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
268	
269	                    target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
270	                        target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
271	                        n_fft=self.n_fft, filter_type=target_ft
272	                    )
273	
274	                    total_loss, components = self.criterion(
275	                        pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
276	                        output["type_logits"], output["H_mag"],
277	                        target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
278	                        target_ft, target_H_mag,
279	                    )
280	
281	                # Skip batch if loss is NaN/Inf to prevent divergence
282	                if not torch.isfinite(total_loss):
283	                    self.global_step += 1
284	                    if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
285	                        print(f"  [train] step={self.global_step} SKIPPING NaN/Inf loss")
286	                    continue
287	
288	                # Scale, backward, clip, update with scaler
289	                self.scaler.scale(total_loss).backward()
290	                self.scaler.unscale_(self.optimizer)
291	                torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0)
292	                self.scaler.step(self.optimizer)
293	                self.scaler.update()
294	            else:
295	                output = self.model(mel_frames)
296	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
297	
298	                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
299	                    target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
300	                    n_fft=self.n_fft, filter_type=target_ft
301	                )
302	
303	                total_loss, components = self.criterion(
304	                    pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
305	                    output["type_logits"], output["H_mag"],
306	                    target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
307	                    target_ft, target_H_mag,
308	                )
309	
310	                # Skip batch if loss is NaN/Inf to prevent divergence
311	                if not torch.isfinite(total_loss):
312	                    self.global_step += 1
313	                    if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
314	                        print(f"  [train] step={self.global_step} SKIPPING NaN/Inf loss")
315	                    continue
316	
317	                total_loss.backward()
318	                torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0)
319	                self.optimizer.step()
320	
321	            epoch_loss += total_loss.item()
322	            n_batches += 1
323	            self.global_step += 1
324	
325	            if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
326	                lr = self.optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
327	                parts = [f"step={self.global_step} loss={total_loss.item():.4f} lr={lr:.2e}"]
328	                for k, v in components.items():
329	                    parts.append(f"{k}={v:.4f}")
330	                print(f"  [train] " + " | ".join(parts))
331	
332	        return epoch_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
333	
334	    @torch.no_grad()
335	    def validate(self, epoch):
336	        self.model.eval()
337	        val_loss = 0.0
338	        n_batches = 0
339	        param_maes = {"gain": [], "freq": [], "q": [], "type_acc": []}
340	
341	        # Use autocast for validation too when AMP is enabled
342	        autocast_ctx = torch.amp.autocast("cuda", enabled=self.use_amp) if self.use_amp else torch.no_grad()
343	
344	        with autocast_ctx:
345	            for batch in self.val_loader:
346	                mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
347	                target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device)
348	                target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device)
349	                target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device)
350	                target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device)
351	
352	                output = self.model(mel_frames)
353	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
354	
355	                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
356	                    target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
357	                    n_fft=self.n_fft, filter_type=target_ft
358	                )
359	
360	                total_loss, _ = self.criterion(
361	                    pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
362	                    output["type_logits"], output["H_mag"],
363	                    target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
364	                    target_ft, target_H_mag,
365	                )
366	
367	                val_loss += total_loss.item()
368	                n_batches += 1
369	
370	                param_maes["gain"].append(
371	                    (pred_gain - target_gain).abs().mean().item()
372	                )
373	                param_maes["freq"].append(
374	                    (torch.log2(pred_freq / (target_freq + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
375	                )
376	                param_maes["q"].append(
377	                    (torch.log10(pred_q / (target_q + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
378	                )
379	                param_maes["type_acc"].append(
380	                    (output["filter_type"] == target_ft).float().mean().item()
381	                )
382	
383	        avg_val_loss = val_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
384	        metrics = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in param_maes.items() if v}
385	
386	        print(
387	            f"  [val] epoch={epoch} val_loss={avg_val_loss:.4f} "
388	            f"gain_mae={metrics.get('gain', 0):.2f}dB "
389	            f"freq_mae={metrics.get('freq', 0):.3f}oct "
390	            f"q_mae={metrics.get('q', 0):.3f}dec "
391	            f"type_acc={metrics.get('type_acc', 0):.1%}"
392	        )
393	        return avg_val_loss, metrics
394	
395	    def save_checkpoint(self, epoch, val_loss, is_best=False, stage_name=""):
396	        ckpt_dir = Path("checkpoints")
397	        ckpt_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
398	        state = {
399	            "epoch": epoch,
400	            "stage": stage_name,
401	            "model_state_dict": self.model.state_dict(),
402	            "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer.state_dict(),
403	            "scheduler_state_dict": self.scheduler.state_dict(),
404	            "val_loss": val_loss,
405	            "best_val_loss": self.best_val_loss,
406	            "global_step": self.global_step,
407	        }
408	        path = ckpt_dir / f"epoch_{epoch:03d}.pt"
409	        torch.save(state, path)
410	        if is_best:
411	            best_path = ckpt_dir / "best.pt"
412	            torch.save(state, best_path)
413	        print(f"  Saved checkpoint: {path}" + (" (best)" if is_best else ""))
414	
415	        # Checkpoint cleanup: keep best.pt + last 3 epoch checkpoints
416	        # Never delete best.pt
417	        epoch_ckpts = sorted(ckpt_dir.glob("epoch_*.pt"))
418	        for old_ckpt in epoch_ckpts[:-3]:  # Keep only last 3
419	            old_ckpt.unlink()
420	            print(f"  Cleaned up old checkpoint: {old_ckpt}")
421	
422	    def _load_checkpoint(self, checkpoint_path):
423	        """Load checkpoint and restore training state."""
424	        print(f"  Loading checkpoint from {checkpoint_path}")
425	        checkpoint = torch.load(checkpoint_path, map_location=self.device, weights_only=True)
426	
427	        self.model.load_state_dict(checkpoint["model_state_dict"])
428	        self.optimizer.load_state_dict(checkpoint["optimizer_state_dict"])
429	
430	        # Restore scheduler state if available
431	        if "scheduler_state_dict" in checkpoint:
432	            self.scheduler.load_state_dict(checkpoint["scheduler_state_dict"])
433	
434	        # Restore training state
435	        self.global_step = checkpoint.get("global_step", 0)
436	        self.best_val_loss = checkpoint.get("best_val_loss", float("inf"))
437	
438	        # Calculate epoch offset for resuming
439	        start_epoch = checkpoint.get("epoch", 0)
440	        self.start_epoch = start_epoch
441	
442	        print(f"  Resumed from epoch {start_epoch}, global_step={self.global_step}")
443	        print(f"  Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
444	
445	    def fit(self):
446	        total_params = sum(p.numel() for p in self.model.parameters())
447	        print(f"IDSP Multi-Type EQ Estimator Training")
448	        print(f"  Device: {self.device}")
449	        print(f"  Parameters: {total_params:,}")
450	        print(f"  Max epochs: {self.max_epochs}")
451	        print(f"  Receptive field: {self.model.receptive_field_frames} frames")
452	
453	        if self.stages:
454	            print(f"  Curriculum: {len(self.stages)} stages")
455	            for s in self.stages:
456	                print(f"    - {s['name']}: {s['epochs']} epochs")
457	
458	        # Get starting epoch from resume or default to 0
459	        global_epoch = getattr(self, 'start_epoch', 0)
460	
461	        if self.stages:
462	            # Curriculum-based training
463	            for stage in self.stages:
464	                self._setup_stage(stage)
465	
466	                for local_epoch in range(1, stage["epochs"] + 1):
467	                    global_epoch += 1
468	
469	                    # Skip epochs that were already completed if resuming
470	                    if hasattr(self, 'start_epoch') and global_epoch <= self.start_epoch:
471	                        print(f"  Skipping epoch {global_epoch}/{self.max_epochs} (already completed)")
472	                        # Still need to step scheduler to maintain state
473	                        self.scheduler.step()
474	                        continue
475	
476	                    t0 = time.time()
477	                    train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(global_epoch)
478	                    val_loss, metrics = self.validate(global_epoch)
479	                    self.scheduler.step()
480	                    elapsed = time.time() - t0
481	
482	                    is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
483	                    if is_best:
484	                        self.best_val_loss = val_loss
485	                    self.save_checkpoint(global_epoch, val_loss, is_best, stage["name"])
486	
487	                    self.history.append({
488	                        "epoch": global_epoch,
489	                        "stage": stage["name"],
490	                        "train_loss": train_loss,
491	                        "val_loss": val_loss,
492	                        "metrics": metrics,
493	                        "time_s": elapsed,
494	                    })
495	
496	                    print(
497	                        f"Epoch {global_epoch}/{self.max_epochs} [{stage['name']}] "
498	                        f"({elapsed:.1f}s) train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
499	                    )
500	        else:
501	            # Flat training (no curriculum)
502	            self._setup_flat_training()
503	            self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
504	                self.optimizer, T_max=self.max_epochs, eta_min=1e-6
505	            )
506	
507	            start_epoch = getattr(self, 'start_epoch', 0)
508	            for epoch in range(start_epoch + 1, self.max_epochs + 1):
509	                t0 = time.time()
510	                train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(epoch)
511	                val_loss, metrics = self.validate(epoch)
512	                self.scheduler.step()
513	                elapsed = time.time() - t0
514	
515	                is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
516	                if is_best:
517	                    self.best_val_loss = val_loss
518	                self.save_checkpoint(epoch, val_loss, is_best)
519	
520	                self.history.append({
521	                    "epoch": epoch,
522	                    "train_loss": train_loss,
523	                    "val_loss": val_loss,
524	                    "metrics": metrics,
525	                    "time_s": elapsed,
526	                })
527	
528	                print(
529	                    f"Epoch {epoch}/{self.max_epochs} ({elapsed:.1f}s) "
530	                    f"train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
531	                )
532	
533	        hist_path = Path("checkpoints") / "training_history.json"
534	        with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
535	            json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
536	        print(f"\nTraining complete. Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
537	        print(f"History saved to {hist_path}")
538	
539	    def _setup_flat_training(self):
540	        """Setup single-stage training (no curriculum)."""
541	        data_cfg = self.data_cfg
542	
543	        # Read duration and size from config instead of hardcoding
544	        duration = data_cfg.get("audio_duration", 1.5)
545	        size = data_cfg.get("dataset_size", 50000)
546	
547	        dataset = SyntheticEQDataset(
548	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
549	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
550	            duration=duration,
551	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
552	            size=size,
553	            gain_range=tuple(data_cfg["gain_bounds"]),
554	            freq_range=tuple(data_cfg["freq_bounds"]),
555	            q_range=tuple(data_cfg["q_bounds"]),
556	            type_weights=data_cfg.get("type_weights", None),
557	            precompute_mels=True,
558	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
559	        )
560	        dataset.precompute()
561	
562	        n_train = int(len(dataset) * data_cfg["train_split"])
563	        n_val = int(len(dataset) * data_cfg["val_split"])
564	        n_test = len(dataset) - n_train - n_val
565	
566	        # Seeded generator for reproducible splits
567	        gen = torch.Generator().manual_seed(self.cfg.get("seed", 42))
568	        train_set, val_set, test_set = torch.utils.data.random_split(
569	            dataset, [n_train, n_val, n_test], generator=gen
570	        )
571	
572	        # Read num_workers from config instead of hardcoding 0
573	        num_workers = data_cfg.get("num_workers", 0)
574	        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
575	
576	        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
577	            train_set,
578	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
579	            shuffle=True,
580	            num_workers=num_workers,
581	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
582	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
583	            drop_last=True,
584	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
585	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
586	        )
587	        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
588	            val_set,
589	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
590	            shuffle=False,
591	            num_workers=num_workers,
592	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
593	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
594	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
595	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
596	        )
597	
598	
599	if __name__ == "__main__":
600	    trainer = Trainer()
601	    trainer.fit()
602
